resolved_subsector: Diversified Indian pharmaceutical company with strong domestic branded formulations (acute, chronic, specialty), consumer healthcare (OTC), and growing biologics/specialty injectables portfolio.

sections.heading: Key Regulatory Filings & Approvals
missing_fields: Number of Drug Master Files (DMFs) filed (US/EU)
Number of Drug Master Files (DMFs) approved (US/EU)
Number of Certificates of Suitability (CEPs) filed (EU)
Number of Certificates of Suitability (CEPs) approved (EU)
Number of USFDA Form 483s received (past 3 years)
Number of USFDA Warning Letters received (past 3 years)
Number of CDSCO (India) New Drug Approvals (past 3 years)
Status of major manufacturing site inspections (e.g., USFDA, EDQM, CDSCO) (e.g., 'No Action Indicated', 'Voluntary Action Indicated')
Number of product recalls (past 3 years)
Any ongoing significant regulatory litigation/penalties

reasoning.pe_analyst: A high number of recent USFDA Form 483s or Warning Letters would significantly increase diligence risk, potentially leading to a 'pass' decision if systemic issues are indicated.
reasoning.banker: A history of significant regulatory issues or penalties could make a company a less attractive M&A or financing target, potentially impacting a banker's decision to pursue a deal.
reasoning.credit_analyst: Numerous USFDA Warning Letters or ongoing significant regulatory litigation would be a major red flag, increasing credit risk and potentially leading to a 'pass' on deeper underwriting.
reasoning.consultant: A pattern of manufacturing site compliance issues (e.g., frequent 'Voluntary Action Indicated' statuses) would signal operational inefficiencies and potential future costs, warranting deeper investigation into operational improvements.
